headline: James answered with concrete needs (authority and clearer criteria) while staying unclear on what changes will actually be recognized—this is a low-signal clarity/engagement session, not a vague dip.
summary_bullets: James tied his reduced team-ritual presence directly to the promotion decision: “After the promotion decision, I was not clear on what really needed to change.”
He wants criteria and authority, not comfort: “I want clearer criteria, not reassurance” and “I can own bigger direction, but I need real authority to do it.”
His growth thread funnels into mentoring as a place he still feels useful, but he doubts recognition: “I am not sure the work I am doing is the work that gets recognized.”
understanding_paragraph: James is less present in team rituals since the promotion decision because he lacks clarity on what needs to change. He’s not asking for reassurance; he’s asking for precise criteria and real authority to own bigger direction. Mentoring is the one area where he still feels useful, but he doesn’t yet believe his current work there maps to what gets recognized.

axes.meaning: Energy is down in a specific way tied to authority and presence, worth watching over the next few weeks.
Engagement is meaningfully suppressed by post-decision uncertainty and reduced ritual presence—act on the clarity/authority gap.
Clarity is the defining problem: he’s not clear what needs to change, and that blocks execution and confidence.
Growth is constrained by recognition mismatch; he wants stretch that’s backed by criteria and authority.

brutal_truth_employee: James said, “After the promotion decision, I was not clear on what really needed to change,” and he stayed there. He also drew a hard line on support: “I want clearer criteria, not reassurance,” and “I need real authority to do it.”
brutal_truth_manager: You got strong, specific signals early (“not clear on what really needed to change” and “need real authority”), but you didn’t lock down the concrete mechanics of what authority/criteria would look like. The conversation stayed at the level of needs; the next step is to explore the exact forum and decision points he named so you can translate them into real commitments.

next_actions.action: Confirm with James the exact forum he referenced for clearer criteria, and schedule a 30-minute working session to define the criteria and decision owners.
Draft a one-page “criteria + authority” plan for James’s next quarter, then ask him to edit it until it matches what he means by “real authority.”
Open the next 1:1 by asking James what changed since the last forum definition and what still feels unrecognized in his mentoring work.

watch_for: Within two weeks: if James’s ritual presence stays low and he repeats “not clear” language, treat the clarity gap as unresolved.
Before next 1:1: if James can’t name criteria/authority outcomes tied to recognition, his engagement will keep tracking the recognition mismatch.
